message:
chore(beads): close ft-b35tw — pattern-pack format conformance shipped (3524b5a9c)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -193,7 +193,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-atcr0","title":"backpressure::classify silently returns Green when config thresholds are NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs` at `BackpressureManager::classify` (lines 416-438):

```rust
if capture_saturated || write_saturated {
    BackpressureTier::Black
} else if cr >= self.config.red_capture || wr >= self.config.red_write {
    BackpressureTier::Red
} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {
    BackpressureTier::Yellow
} else {
    BackpressureTier::Green
}
```

Any `>=` comparison with NaN is false. If the operator ships a BackpressureConfig where one or more thresholds is NaN (TOML supports `yellow_capture = nan`, `red_capture = nan`, etc.), every branch falls through to Green. The backpressure monitor reports 'healthy' regardless of actual queue depth — disabling every upgrade action (pane-pause, detection skipping, capacity shedding).

# Parallel to ft-761tz

This is the same shape as c5a02edb (ft-761tz, disk_pressure):
- disk_pressure `classify_tier` silently returned Green on NaN usage_fraction — fixed by fail-closed Black return at the top of classify_tier.
- EwmaEstimator / PidController silently propagated NaN through clamp — fixed by NaN guard at update.

backpressure.rs has not received the same hardening. Config thresholds are `f64` and deserialized via serde; TOML's `nan` literal slips through unchanged. No `validate()` method exists on BackpressureConfig.

# Fix

1. Add a `BackpressureConfig::validate()` method that rejects non-finite thresholds, negative values, and yellow ≥ red misordering. Emit a descriptive error.
2. Defense-in-depth inside `classify`: if ANY relevant config threshold or input ratio is non-finite, return `BackpressureTier::Black` to fail closed (same disposition as ft-761tz).
3. Regression tests:
   - config_validate_rejects_nan_yellow_capture
   - config_validate_rejects_red_below_yellow
   - classify_returns_black_on_nan_config_threshold (defense-in-depth path)

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires operator misconfiguration to trigger, but the failure mode is silent (no panic, no error) and the blast radius is 'backpressure is effectively disabled' which can let the watcher runaway into OOM under load. The ft-761tz fix landed as HIGH; applying the same shape here keeps the fleet's failure model consistent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:03:47.755984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773267Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): NaN-proof backpressure)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-auwbi","title":"rch verifier fails: openssl-sys build/main.rs:316 missing /opt/homebrew clang on Linux worker","description":"Verifier command:
`rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p4-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib pre_start_cancelled_cx_fails_execution_and_releases_lock`

Observed failure:
- `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- cargo surfaced: `error: failed to run custom build command for openssl-sys v0.9.112`

This is a worker-env mismatch: the requested macOS Homebrew clang path does not exist on the Linux rch worker, so verification fails before reaching frankenterm-core test code.","notes":"2026-04-22 tick-5 backpressure verifier:
- command: `rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p8-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib classify_small_write_capacity_requires_high_fill_for_black`
- worker: `vmi1149989`
- cargo failed before frankenterm-core with `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `/home/ubuntu/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/openssl-sys-0.9.112/build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:12:32.293332Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.871320Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.870997Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 31bad457","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-azjol","title":"[sec] ipc.rs: non-constant-time token compare (HIGH)","description":"CVSS v3.1 AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N ~5.7. Fixed in 5810a3a4. IpcAuth::authorize() used String::eq which short-circuits on first byte mismatch, plus .find() which short-circuits on first candidate match. Both are exploitable via timing side-channel over Unix socket: co-tenant process can recover the stored IPC token byte-by-byte AND distinguish token position in the vec. Fix: constant-time byte XOR accumulator in ipc_constant_time_eq + explicit loop in authorize() that visits every candidate. 7 regression tests. Found during /security-audit-for-saas adapted for CLI control-plane (pane6 phase C).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:27.060545Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327699Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5810a3a4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-b35tw","title":"[conformance] pattern-pack file format has no spec, no JSON-Schema, no fixture corpus from a reference loader","description":"## The gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` parses YAML and JSON
pattern-pack manifests (built-in core packs, user packs, nested
`.ft/patterns/` discovery). The pack format is a public surface that
external rule authors and security auditors interact with, but there is no:

1. Authoritative spec document (no `docs/patterns-pack-format.md`,
   no entry in `docs/proposals/`).
2. JSON Schema or YAML schema published alongside the parser.
3. Reference fixture corpus pinning known-good packs across schema
   versions.
4. Spec-derived test matrix enumerating MUST/SHOULD clauses for the
   format.

## What exists today

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns.rs` (52 KB) —
  excellent property tests for *engine behavior*.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs` —
  metamorphic relations on triggering.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/pattern_corpus.rs` — a runtime
  corpus, not a format-conformance corpus.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs`,
  `metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs` — behavior, not format.

None of these enforce format-version compatibility, validate that a
pack hand-authored against the format produces the documented Rust
struct, or guard against silent breaking changes when fields are
renamed.

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

External rule authors today must read `patterns.rs` source to learn
the schema. When fields are renamed, deprecated, or moved between
nested manifests:

- Hand-authored packs in the wild break silently (parser drops
  unknown fields under default serde behavior).
- The `discover_packs_from_dir` symlink-escape fix (recent commit
  `6ab52765`) shows pack-loading is a security-sensitive surface;
  format drift compounds the risk surface.
- The `nested yaml/json pack manifests` discovery (`07e72a52`) is
  exactly the kind of feature where the format spec should pin
  the contract before more loaders depend on it.

A conformance harness with a reference corpus closes both holes:
external authors read the spec; CI catches drift between spec and
loader.

## Suggested implementation

1. **Format spec** (`docs/patterns-pack-format.md`):
   - Top-level structure (`name`, `version`, `format_version`,
     `rules`, etc.).
   - Each `Rule` field with type, presence (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), and
     example.
   - Versioning policy: how a `format_version` bump is signaled,
     what's backward-compatible, what's a breaking change.
   - Discovery rules (built-in vs `.ft/patterns/` vs user packs)
     and the symlink-escape sandbox guarantees.

2. **JSON Schema** (`docs/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json`):
   - Generate via `schemars` from the existing `Pack` / `Rule`
     structs (consistent with the strategy in `api_schema.rs`).
   - Add a row to `docs/json-schema/PROVENANCE.md` (per ft-d5268).

3. **Reference fixture corpus**
   (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/pattern_packs/`):
   - One subdirectory per supported `format_version`.
   - Each contains 5–10 hand-authored YAML+JSON pack examples that
     exercise every documented field.
   - Include intentionally-malformed packs (in
     `fixtures/pattern_packs/invalid/`) with the expected error
     class for each.

4. **Conformance test** (`tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs`):
   - Loads every fixture pack, asserts the expected parse outcome.
   - Validates each accepted fixture against the JSON Schema.
   - Round-trips: parse → serialize → parse-equal-to-original (for
     formats that should round-trip).
   - Asserts every rule field documented in the spec is reachable
     from at least one fixture (coverage matrix in stdout).

## Out of scope

- Authoring new pattern rules (this is purely format infrastructure).
- The Aho-Corasick / regex engine layer (already covered by existing
  proptest suites).
- Runtime caching of compiled packs (recent perf work in
  `d05e5b7b`).

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/patterns-pack-format.md` exists and is referenced from
  README's "Pattern Rules Tooling" section.
- A renamed field in `Rule` without a fixture+spec update fails the
  conformance test.
- A new fixture in `fixtures/pattern_packs/` whose schema validates
  but whose semantics violate a rule loader invariant produces a
  precise diagnostic, not silent acceptance.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` — the loader
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/pattern_trigger.rs` — the engine
- Recent format-touching commits: `6ab52765` (symlink escape),
  `07e72a52` (nested manifest discovery), `0239fbd6` (HIGH:
  canonicalize), `37384b01` (sandbox_resolve_dir)

Filed by cc_1 conformance audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:37:32.232686Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:18:41.776195Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

1. Authoritative spec document (no `docs/patterns-pack-format.md`,

4. **Conformance test** (`tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs`):
   - Loads every fixture pack, asserts the expected parse outcome.

     formats that should round-trip).

     from at least one fixture (coverage matrix in stdout).

- Authoring new pattern rules (this is purely format infrastructure).
- The Aho-Corasick / regex engine layer (already covered by existing

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` — the loader

Filed by cc_1 conformance audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:37:32.232686Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:31:53.178612Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T10:31:53.178392Z","close_reason":"Pattern-pack format conformance shipped at 3524b5a9c. 4 deliverables: (1) docs/patterns-pack-format.md — authoritative spec with top-level + Rule fields, MUST/SHOULD/MAY presence per RFC 2119, discovery+sandbox section citing 6ab52765 symlink-escape fix, versioning policy. (2) docs/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json — Draft 2020-12 schema hand-authored from PatternPack (patterns.rs:619) + RuleDef (:487), additionalProperties:false on both layers, snake_case enums for AgentType+Severity. (3) tests/fixtures/pattern_packs/{valid,invalid}/ — 4 valid (minimal yaml, full yaml exercising every field, multi-rule json, toml round-trip) + 3 invalid (duplicate id, missing severity, empty name). (4) tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs — 7 tests covering schema-compile, schema-validates-fixtures, loader-parity, invalid-rejection, RuleDef-coverage matrix, plus 2 falsification tests that prove additionalProperties:false fires. Regression-injection meta-test: bogus rule field flips both schema gate AND coverage matrix red; restoration returns all 7 to green. Mirrors ft-5ikbd's jsonschema validator infra and ft-zaqi8's coverage matrix pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b4kpu","title":"Fix no-default-features lib blockers in search_explain and storage FTS helpers","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:16.262625Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:26:53.198935Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:26:53.198610Z","close_reason":"Stale failure snapshot: current main is internally consistent again after 1ad5c8cc landed the storage-side FTS rename that was racing the verifier.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b4l62","title":"[MEDIUM] telemetry Histogram NaN-poisons total_sum/mean and emits nonsense quantiles on NaN q","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure paths

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs`, `struct Histogram`.

### 1. `Histogram::record(NaN)` permanently poisons the running mean

```rust
pub fn record(&mut self, value: f64) {
    self.total_count += 1;
    self.total_sum += value;    // NaN poisons total_sum permanently
    if value < self.min { self.min = value; }
    if value > self.max { self.max = value; }
    ...
}
```

Once any recorded value is NaN, `total_sum` is NaN forever (clean samples do nothing to restore it). Then `mean()` returns `Some(NaN)`:

```rust
pub fn mean(&self) -> Option<f64> {
    if self.total_count == 0 { return None; }
    Some(self.total_sum / self.total_count as f64)
}
```

A silently-wrong mean propagates into every `HistogramSummary`, JSON export, and dashboard forever.

### 2. `Histogram::quantile(NaN)` returns an arbitrary sample

```rust
let idx = ((sorted.len() as f64 - 1.0) * q.clamp(0.0, 1.0)) as usize;
```

`f64::clamp(NaN, 0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN (documented gotcha). `(NaN) as usize` is 0 in Rust. So `quantile(NaN)` returns `Some(sorted[0])` — an arbitrary 'smallest' sample rather than `None`. Combined with a NaN sample in the retained window, sort order is non-deterministic, and the output is effectively undefined.

### 3. Classify: silent wrong-answer

Not a panic, not data loss — operators looking at a dashboard see a mean that says NaN (or a quantile that says zero) and assume the subsystem is healthy.

## Reachability

`Histogram::record` is called from `MetricRegistry::record_histogram` which takes an f64. Callers that pass `dt.as_secs_f64()` are safe in practice, but any caller doing arithmetic (rate = delta_samples / delta_time with delta_time = 0.0) can produce NaN. Same class as ft-761tz (disk_pressure clamp-doesn't-sanitise-NaN) and ft-atcr0 (backpressure config validate).

## Fix

1. `Histogram::record`: if `value.is_nan()`, return early (no counter bump, no sum update, no sample push). This matches ft-761tz's fail-closed precedent.
2. `Histogram::quantile`: if `q.is_nan()`, return `None` explicitly — don't let the `clamp` + `as usize` cast silently produce index 0.
3. Regression tests covering both defenses.

## Scope

Single file (`telemetry.rs`). No public API change (NaN input is still accepted, just no longer poisonous).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:39:42.897340Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:08.381499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:08.380804Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b4mif","title":"[review][MEDIUM] README Quickstart claims ft --version emits rustc/target/features but it only emits semver+git","description":"README Quickstart section (landed in bcc668a0, Apr 23 13:54) claims:

  ft --version                 # semver + git commit + rustc + target + features

The clap version wiring at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:67-72 binds version = CLAP_VERSION.as_str() where CLAP_VERSION = build_meta::short_version() (lines 39-41). short_version() returns only:

  format!("{} ({}{})", frankenterm_core::VERSION, GIT_HASH, GIT_DIRTY)

i.e. semver + git commit + optional dirty marker. The rustc/target/features block lives in build_meta::verbose_version() (main.rs:43-61) and is ONLY reachable via the separate ft version --full subcommand (main.rs:16718-16724), NOT ft --version.

Without #[command(long_version = ...)] in the clap derive, ft --version and ft -V both surface short_version() only. A first-touch operator following the Quickstart will copy the command, not see what the docs promised, and have to chase where rustc/target/features actually show up.

Fix options:
1. Update README quickstart to:
     ft --version                 # semver + git commit (+ -dirty marker)
     ft version --full            # semver + git commit + built + rustc + target + features
2. OR add long_version = build_meta::verbose_version() to the Cli derive at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:72 so ft --version actually emits the full block (ft -V stays short).

Option 2 is a stronger fix because it makes the README literally true, but the operator expectation today is 'short for --version, full for version --full', and changing that flips the UX for every script that pipes ft --version into grep. Option 1 (doc fix) is safer.

Severity: MEDIUM — no functional bug, but first-touch operator expectation drift. Discovered in reviewer pane audit round 2, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:52.738804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:33.492364Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:33.492043Z","close_reason":"Doc fix path (b): README Quickstart step 2 now shows 'ft --version' for semver+short-git+dirty and 'ft version --full' for built/rustc/target/features, matching what crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs actually emits (short_version at :39-41, verbose_version at :43-61, version --full handler at :16718-16724). Binary behavior untouched so scripts grepping ft --version keep the short form. Landed in 86e08660.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
